question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker front-run a pending bundle transaction by calling Midnight.setConsumed(offer.group, type(uint256).max, offer.maker) on behalf of an authorized maker, under the precondition that the attacker is authorized by the maker (or the maker has authorized the attacker), causing the bundle's ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits() to return 0 for every offer in takes[], so every try-catch take silently skips, and the final require(filledUnits == targetUnits, OutOfOffers()) reverts, violating the invariant that a valid bundle with sufficient offers must complete, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for the victim taker? Proof idea: fuzz offer.group and maker authorization state; assert that setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max) before the bundle call always triggers OutOfOffers().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker cause a permanent DoS of the entire bundle by supplying a single offer in takes[] whose tick maps to a buyerPrice > WAD (i.e., tick such that TickLib.tickToPrice(tick) + settlementFee > WAD), under the precondition that the offer is a sell offer (offer.buy == false) and the bundler calls ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits which internally calls TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits which reverts with PriceGreaterThanOne(), causing the entire bundle to revert (not caught by try-catch since the revert happens before the try block), violating the invariant that a single bad offer should not block all other valid offers, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for all takers using this bundle? Proof idea: unit test with tick = 0 (price near 1) and non-zero settlementFee; assert bundle reverts before any take executes.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the assets-target buy bundle by inserting a crafted offer with tick such that buyerPrice > WAD into the takes[] array, under the precondition that the attacker controls one offer slot in the takes array passed by the taker (e.g., via a shared routing layer or by being the maker of a valid-looking offer), causing TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits to revert with PriceGreaterThanOne() before the try block, reverting the entire bundle, violating the invariant that invalid offers should be skippable, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for the victim taker? Proof idea: fuzz offer.tick near the WAD boundary; assert that any tick where tickToPrice(tick) + settlementFee > WAD causes full bundle revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by front-running each offer's consumption just enough to reduce consumableUnits to a value that, when summed across all offers, is less than targetUnits, under the precondition that the attacker monitors the mempool and partially fills each offer (consuming exactly takes[i].units - epsilon units per offer) before the bundle executes, causing the loop to exhaust all offers with filledUnits < targetUnits and revert with OutOfOffers(), violating the invariant that a bundle with sufficient listed offers must succeed, causing scoped impact: DoS of take with low attacker cost (only gas + dust fills)? Proof idea: fuzz partial consumption amounts across N offers; assert that consuming (targetUnits - 1) total units across all offers before the bundle always triggers OutOfOffers().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS a borrower's repayment by causing the subsequent withdrawCollateral call to revert (e.g., by temporarily making the borrower's position unhealthy via oracle price manipulation within the same block, or by the oracle reverting), under the precondition that the borrower has debt and collateral, and the collateralWithdrawals array is non-empty, causing the entire repayAndWithdrawCollateral transaction to revert (including the repay state change), violating the invariant that a valid repay must always succeed independently of collateral withdrawal, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay for the borrower? Proof idea: unit test where withdrawCollateral reverts due to UnhealthyBorrower after repay; assert the entire tx reverts and debt is unchanged.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the collateral withdrawal step of the bundle by supplying a collateralWithdrawals entry with a collateralIndex that references a collateral whose oracle is currently reverting, under the precondition that the taker has debt and the oracle for collateralIndex is reverting, causing withdrawCollateral to revert (since isHealthy calls the oracle), which reverts the entire bundle including the already-executed takes, violating the invariant that successful takes must not be rolled back by a subsequent collateral withdrawal failure, causing scoped impact: DoS of take and collateral withdrawal? Proof idea: mock oracle that reverts; assert bundle reverts after takes succeed.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker cause a permanent DoS of the assets-target buy bundle by exploiting a rounding mismatch between TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits (which computes units from target assets) and Midnight.take (which computes assets from units), under the precondition that the settlement fee and tick price create a rounding scenario where the forward computation (units -> assets) does not exactly reproduce targetFilledBuyerAssets, causing the require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets, OutOfOffers()) to always revert for a specific targetBuyerAssets value, violating the invariant that the inverse function must be a perfect left-inverse of the forward function, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for specific asset amounts? Proof idea: differential fuzz test comparing TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits(x) then take's forward computation; assert they are always equal.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker cause a permanent DoS of the assets-target sell bundle by exploiting a rounding mismatch between TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits and Midnight.take's forward sellerAssets computation, under the precondition that the offer is a buy offer (offer.buy == true) and the sellerPrice and WAD create a mulDivUp/mulDivDown asymmetry, causing require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets, OutOfOffers()) to always revert, violating the invariant that the inverse function must reproduce the exact target, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for specific seller asset targets? Proof idea: differential fuzz test sellerAssetsToUnits vs take's sellerAssets computation across all tick values.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker cause an arithmetic underflow DoS in the final token refund by crafting a scenario where referralFeeAssets > maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets, under the precondition that referralFeePct is close to WAD-1 and filledBuyerAssets is close to maxBuyerAssets, causing the computation maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets to underflow and revert, violating the invariant that the bundle must always return excess tokens to msg.sender, causing scoped impact: DoS of take and fund freeze (tokens stuck in bundler)? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD and filledBuyerAssets near maxBuyerAssets; assert underflow condition.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker cause an arithmetic underflow DoS by passing referralFeePct such that referralFeeAssets > targetBuyerAssets, under the precondition that targetBuyerAssets is small and referralFeePct is close to WAD-1, causing the computation targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets (line: uint256 targetFilledBuyerAssets = targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) to underflow and revert before any take executes, violating the invariant that valid referralFeePct < WAD must not cause underflow, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: unit test with targetBuyerAssets = 1 and referralFeePct = WAD - 1; assert underflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker cause an arithmetic overflow DoS by passing targetSellerAssets and referralFeePct such that targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets overflows uint256, under the precondition that targetSellerAssets is near type(uint256).max and referralFeePct > 0, causing the computation targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets to overflow and revert, violating the invariant that valid inputs must not cause overflow, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets near type(uint256).max with referralFeePct > 0; assert overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker cause a panic DoS by calling any bundle function with an empty takes[] array, under the precondition that takes.length == 0, causing the array access takes[0].offer.market.loanToken to revert with a panic (index out of bounds), violating the invariant that the bundler must handle empty input gracefully, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: unit test with takes = new Take[](0); assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by front-running the touchMarket call to change the market's settlement fee (via feeSetter), causing the settlement fee used in ConsumableUnitsLib/TakeAmountsLib to differ from the fee used in the actual take, under the precondition that the feeSetter changes the settlement fee between the bundle's touchMarket and the take calls, causing TakeAmountsLib to compute wrong units that don't match the actual take's asset computation, causing require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) to revert, violating the invariant that settlement fee must be consistent within a bundle, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: state-transition test where feeSetter changes fee between touchMarket and take; assert mismatch.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS a borrower's repay-and-withdraw by supplying a collateralWithdrawals entry with collateralIndex pointing to a collateral that the borrower has not activated (collateral[collateralIndex] == 0), under the precondition that the borrower has debt and the collateralWithdrawals array references an unactivated index, causing withdrawCollateral to attempt to subtract assets from a zero balance and revert with arithmetic underflow, violating the invariant that valid repay must succeed independently of collateral withdrawal parameters, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay? Proof idea: unit test with collateralWithdrawals[0].assets > 0 and position.collateral[index] == 0; assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the sell bundle by supplying a collateralSupplies entry with a collateralIndex that causes TooManyActivatedCollaterals() to revert in supplyCollateral, under the precondition that the taker already has MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) activated collaterals and the collateralSupplies array adds a new one, causing the entire bundle to revert before any take executes, violating the invariant that collateral supply failure must not block the take flow, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: unit test with 16 pre-activated collaterals; assert TooManyActivatedCollaterals on bundle call.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by ensuring all offers in takes[] have unitsToTake == 0 (because consumableUnits returns 0 for each), under the precondition that all offers are fully consumed, causing each take(offer, ratifierData, 0, ...) to be a no-op (Midnight allows no-ops), filledUnits never increases, and the loop exhausts all offers with filledUnits == 0 < targetUnits, reverting with OutOfOffers(), violating the invariant that a bundle with targetUnits > 0 must fill at least some units, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: fuzz all offers as fully consumed; assert OutOfOffers().

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: forceApproveMax (internal)] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by deploying a malicious ERC20 token as the loan token that reverts on approve(spender, 0) when the current allowance is non-zero, under the precondition that the bundler has a non-zero but less than type(uint96).max/2 allowance for MIDNIGHT, causing forceApproveMax to call safeApprove(token, MIDNIGHT, 0) which reverts, blocking all bundle functions that use this loan token, violating the invariant that token approval must not block core actions, causing scoped impact: DoS of take, repay? Proof idea: mock token that reverts on approve(0) with non-zero allowance; assert bundle DoS.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: pullToken (internal, ERC2612 path)] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by front-running the ERC2612 permit call to consume the permit nonce before the bundle executes, under the precondition that the attacker observes the permit signature in the mempool and calls token.permit() directly, causing the bundler's try-catch permit call to silently succeed (no revert), but the subsequent safeTransferFrom to revert due to insufficient allowance (since the permit was consumed but the allowance was set for the attacker, not the bundler), violating the invariant that a valid permit must enable the bundle to pull tokens, causing scoped impact: DoS of take, repay? Proof idea: front-run permit with attacker address; assert safeTransferFrom reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: pullToken (internal, Permit2 path)] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by replaying or front-running the Permit2 signature to consume the nonce before the bundle executes, under the precondition that the attacker observes the Permit2 signature in the mempool and calls IPermit2.permitTransferFrom directly, causing the bundler's Permit2 call to revert with InvalidNonce(), which is not caught, reverting the entire bundle, violating the invariant that a valid Permit2 signature must enable the bundle to pull tokens, causing scoped impact: DoS of take, repay? Proof idea: front-run Permit2 nonce; assert bundle reverts with InvalidNonce.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the sell bundle by supplying a collateralSupplies entry where the permit for the collateral token is a Permit2 signature that has already been consumed, under the precondition that the attacker front-runs the Permit2 nonce for the collateral token, causing pullToken to revert with InvalidNonce() before any collateral is supplied or any take executes, violating the invariant that a valid bundle must not be blockable by permit front-running, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: front-run collateral Permit2 nonce; assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by ensuring that the InconsistentMarket check (require(toId(takes[i].offer.market) == id)) reverts for a crafted offer in takes[], under the precondition that the attacker controls one offer slot and provides an offer with a different market (different loanToken or maturity), causing the require to revert (not caught by try-catch since it's outside the try block), reverting the entire bundle, violating the invariant that market inconsistency should be skippable, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: insert one offer with wrong market in takes[]; assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the bundle by ensuring that the InconsistentSide check (require(!takes[i].offer.buy)) reverts for a crafted offer in takes[], under the precondition that the attacker controls one offer slot and provides a buy offer (offer.buy == true) in a buy bundle, causing the require to revert outside the try-catch block, reverting the entire bundle, violating the invariant that side inconsistency should be skippable, causing scoped impact: DoS of take? Proof idea: insert one buy offer in a buy bundle's takes[]; assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS a borrower's repay by causing the Midnight.repay call to revert due to the borrower's debt being less than units (arithmetic underflow in position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units), under the precondition that the attacker front-runs the bundle to partially repay the borrower's debt (reducing it below the bundle's units parameter), causing the repay to revert with arithmetic underflow, violating the invariant that a valid repay amount must succeed, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay? Proof idea: front-run with partial repay reducing debt below bundle's units; assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS a borrower's repay-and-withdraw by supplying a collateralWithdrawals array that, after repay, leaves the position unhealthy (e.g., withdrawing more collateral than the remaining debt allows), under the precondition that the borrower's position is borderline healthy and the collateral withdrawal amount is too large, causing withdrawCollateral to revert with UnhealthyBorrower(), which reverts the entire bundle including the repay, violating the invariant that repay must succeed independently of collateral withdrawal health check, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay? Proof idea: unit test with collateral withdrawal that makes position unhealthy post-repay; assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker DoS the collateral withdrawal step by front-running to supply dust collateral to the taker's position at a new collateralIndex, activating a new collateral whose oracle is reverting, under the precondition that the attacker is authorized to supply collateral on behalf of the taker (or the taker's authorization is open), causing isHealthy to call the reverting oracle during withdrawCollateral, reverting the entire bundle, violating the invariant that collateral supply by a third party must not block the taker's withdrawals, causing scoped impact: DoS of withdraw and take? Proof idea: supply dust collateral with reverting oracle; assert withdrawCollateral reverts.
